subject: Physical Education
chapter: Physical Education and Sports for CWSN

definitions.definition: Children with special needs refer to those who have physical, mental, or emotional disabilities that require special care and attention. They may have conditions such as autism, cerebral palsy, or Down syndrome, and may need adapted physical education programs to participate fully.
definitions.example: A child with cerebral palsy may require the use of a wheelchair or assistive device to participate in physical education classes.

definitions.definition: Inclusive education refers to the practice of educating children with special needs alongside their non-disabled peers, with adaptations and support as needed. This approach values diversity and promotes social inclusion.
definitions.example: An inclusive physical education class might include children with and without disabilities, with the teacher providing modified activities to meet the needs of all students.

definitions.definition: Disability refers to a physical, mental, or emotional condition that limits a person's ability to perform certain activities or participate fully in society. Disabilities can be visible or invisible, and may require accommodations or support to enable participation.
definitions.example: A person with a visual impairment may require assistive technology, such as a screen reader, to access digital information.

definitions.definition: Impairment refers to a loss or limitation of physical, mental, or emotional function. Impairments can be temporary or permanent, and may or may not result in a disability.
definitions.example: A person with a broken leg may have a temporary impairment, but may not be considered disabled if they can still participate in most activities with accommodations.

definitions.definition: Handicap refers to a social or environmental barrier that prevents a person with an impairment or disability from participating fully in society. Handicaps can be physical, attitudinal, or institutional.
definitions.example: A building without wheelchair ramps or elevators creates a physical handicap for people with mobility impairments.

definitions.definition: Accessible sports refer to physical activities that have been adapted to include people with disabilities. These adaptations may include modified rules, equipment, or facilities.
definitions.example: Wheelchair basketball is an example of an accessible sport, where players use wheelchairs and the court is designed to accommodate them.

definitions.definition: Adapted physical education refers to physical education programs that have been modified to meet the needs of children with special needs. APE programs may include adapted activities, equipment, and teaching methods.
definitions.example: An APE program for children with visual impairments might include activities such as beep baseball or goalball, which use sound to facilitate participation.

definitions.definition: An individualized education program (IEP) is a personalized plan developed for a child with special needs, outlining their goals, objectives, and accommodations. IEPs are typically developed by a team of educators, family members, and healthcare professionals.
definitions.example: A child with autism may have an IEP that includes goals for social interaction, communication, and behavior management, as well as accommodations such as extra time to complete assignments.

definitions.definition: The least restrictive environment (LRE) refers to the educational setting that provides the most opportunities for social interaction and learning, while also meeting the needs of the child with special needs. LRE may include general education classrooms, special education classrooms, or a combination of both.
definitions.example: A child with a physical disability may be placed in a general education classroom with accommodations, such as a wheelchair-accessible desk and adaptive equipment.

definitions.definition: Mainstreaming refers to the practice of placing children with special needs in general education classrooms, with support and accommodations as needed. Mainstreaming aims to promote social inclusion and equal access to education.
definitions.example: A child with a learning disability may be mainstreamed into a general education classroom, with a special education teacher providing additional support and accommodations.

definitions.definition: Para sports refer to competitive sports that have been adapted for people with disabilities. Para sports may include events such as wheelchair racing, blind football, or deaf basketball.
definitions.example: The Paralympic Games feature a range of para sports, including track and field, swimming, and table tennis, with athletes competing in different disability categories.

definitions.definition: Rehabilitation refers to the process of helping individuals with disabilities to regain or develop skills, abilities, and independence. Rehabilitation may involve physical therapy, occupational therapy, or other forms of support.
definitions.example: A person who has had a stroke may undergo rehabilitation to regain mobility, speech, and cognitive function.

definitions.definition: Special Olympic sports refer to competitive sports programs designed for people with intellectual disabilities. Special Olympic sports aim to promote physical fitness, social interaction, and self-esteem.
definitions.example: Special Olympics offers a range of sports, including athletics, swimming, and basketball, with competitions at local, national, and international levels.

definitions.definition: Sports for people with disabilities refer to physical activities that have been adapted or designed to include people with disabilities. These sports may include competitive events, recreational activities, or fitness programs.
definitions.example: Sports such as wheelchair tennis, blind cricket, or deaf football provide opportunities for people with disabilities to participate in physical activity and compete with others.

definitions.definition: Therapeutic recreation refers to the use of recreational activities to promote physical, emotional, and social well-being for people with disabilities. Therapeutic recreation may involve adapted sports, games, or leisure activities.
definitions.example: A therapeutic recreation program for people with physical disabilities might include activities such as adaptive yoga, swimming, or horseback riding, designed to promote relaxation, mobility, and social interaction.

definitions.definition: Universal design refers to the practice of designing products, environments, and services to be accessible and usable by everyone, regardless of ability or disability. Universal design aims to promote inclusivity and equal access.
definitions.example: A public park with wheelchair-accessible pathways, audio signals at crosswalks, and Braille signage is an example of universal design, as it provides equal access to all users.
